Here’s how the **Figma plugin** works:

---

## What the plugin is

The Figma plugin adds:

1. **Two MCP servers** – `figma` and `figma-desktop` – that talk to Figma (cloud and desktop app).
2. **Three skills** – workflows the agent can run when you use certain phrases or `/` commands.

---

## MCP servers

| Server | Purpose |
|--------|--------|
| **figma** | Uses Figma **cloud** – you pass a Figma URL (with `node-id`) so the agent can read that file/node. |
| **figma-desktop** | Uses the **Figma desktop app** – the agent uses the **currently selected node** in the open file, so you don’t need to paste a URL. |

Both expose tools such as:

- `get_design_context` – layout, typography, colors, structure for a node  
- `get_screenshot` – image of the design for visual reference  
- `get_metadata` – high-level node tree (useful for big files)  
- `get_code_connect_suggestions` / `send_code_connect_mappings` – Code Connect (design ↔ code mapping)  
- `create_design_system_rules` – template for project-specific Figma→code rules  

**Authentication:** The **figma** server needs to be authenticated. Its `STATUS.md` says: *“Authenticate it by calling the `mcp_auth` tool for server ‘plugin-figma-figma’.”* So before using “implement design” or “code connect” with a URL, you may need to run that auth step once.

---

## Skills (how to trigger them)

You can trigger the skills by **saying something in chat** or by using **`/`** and picking the skill.

### 1. **Implement design**

- **When:** You say things like “implement this design”, “build this component”, “implement design”, or you give a Figma URL.
- **What it does:**  
  - Gets design context + screenshot for the node (from URL or from desktop selection).  
  - Optionally gets metadata and child nodes if the design is large.  
  - Downloads assets (icons, images) from Figma.  
  - Translates the design into your project’s stack (e.g. React + your design system), aiming for 1:1 look and behavior.  
  - Validates against the Figma screenshot before finishing.

### 2. **Code Connect components**

- **When:** You say “code connect”, “connect this component to code”, “map this component”, “link component to code”, etc.
- **What it does:**  
  - Finds **unmapped** Figma components (from URL or desktop selection).  
  - Scans your codebase for matching components (by name, props, structure).  
  - Suggests mappings (Figma component ↔ code file).  
  - After you confirm, sends the mappings to Figma via `send_code_connect_mappings`.  

**Requirements:** The Figma component must be **published to a team library**, and Code Connect needs an **Organization/Enterprise** plan.

### 3. **Create design system rules**

- **When:** You say “create design system rules”, “generate rules for my project”, “set up design rules”, etc.
- **What it does:**  
  - Calls `create_design_system_rules` (with your languages/frameworks) to get a template.  
  - Looks at your repo (component folders, styling, tokens, patterns).  
  - Writes project-specific rules (e.g. where components live, tokens, Figma→code flow, assets).  
  - Saves them in the right place for your agent (e.g. Cursor: `.cursor/rules/figma-design-system.mdc`).  

After that, the agent follows these rules whenever it implements Figma designs in this project.

---

## Flow in practice

1. **Implement a screen/component**  
   - Share a Figma URL with `node-id`, or select the node in the Figma desktop app.  
   - Say e.g. “Implement this design” or use the implement-design skill.  
   - The agent uses `get_design_context` + `get_screenshot` (and assets), then generates code that matches your project and checks it against the screenshot.

2. **Connect Figma to code (Code Connect)**  
   - Point to a frame/component (URL or selection in desktop).  
   - Say “Connect this to code” or use the code-connect skill.  
   - The agent suggests which code files map to which Figma components and, after you confirm, sends the mappings to Figma.

3. **Standardize how Figma is implemented**  
   - Say “Create design system rules” (or similar).  
   - The agent creates/updates rules (e.g. in `.cursor/rules/figma-design-system.mdc`) so future “implement design” runs follow your conventions automatically.

---

## Quick reference

| Goal | Say or do |
|------|------------|
| Implement a Figma frame/component | Paste Figma URL with `node-id` or select in desktop, then “Implement this design” or use `/` → implement-design. |
| Link Figma components to code | “Code connect” / “Connect this component to code” (with URL or selection). |
| Set project rules for Figma→code | “Create design system rules” or “Set up Figma rules”. |
| Fix “not working” with URLs | Ensure the **figma** MCP server is authenticated (e.g. run `mcp_auth` for `plugin-figma-figma` once). |

If you tell me your setup (e.g. “I use Figma desktop only” or “I only have URLs”), I can give you the exact steps for that case (including auth).

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

So the tools are:
- get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_variable_defs, get_figjam
- get_code_connect_suggestions, send_code_connect_mappings, add_code_connect_map, get_code_connect_map
- create_design_system_rules (creates rules in the codebase, not in Figma)
- mcp_auth

None of these create or edit Figma designs (frames, shapes, text, components) inside Figma. The Figma API does have write capabilities in some contexts (e.g. Figma plugins), but the MCP server exposed here is read-focused + Code Connect mappings. So the honest answer is: No, with this plugin I cannot create or edit designs inside Figma. I can only read from Figma and implement them in code (or connect components to code).
</details></think>

---


**Kurz gesagt: Nein.** Mit dem Figma-Plugin kann ich **keine Designs in Figma erstellen oder bearbeiten**.

Das Plugin und der Figma-MCP-Server können:

- **Aus Figma lesen:** Design-Kontext, Screenshots, Metadaten, Variablen
- **Code Connect:** Figma-Komponenten mit Code-Dateien verknüpfen
- **Design-System-Regeln:** Regeln für dein Projekt (z. B. in `.cursor/rules/`) erzeugen

Es gibt **keine** MCP-Tools zum Erstellen oder Ändern von Frames, Komponenten, Texten, Farben usw. **in** Figma. Die Figma REST API erlaubt das übrigens auch nicht – sie ist überwiegend read-only; Schreibzugriff gibt es nur über Figma-Plugins direkt in der App.

**Was ich mit dem Plugin machen kann:**

| Ja | Nein |
|----|------|
| Dein bestehendes Figma-Design auslesen | Neue Screens/Frames in Figma anlegen |
| Daraus Code (z. B. React) implementieren | Komponenten in Figma erstellen oder anpassen |
| Design ↔ Code per Code Connect verknüpfen | Layout, Farben, Typo in Figma ändern |
| Design-System-Regeln für dein Repo schreiben | Assets/Illustrationen in Figma zeichnen |

Designs in Figma musst du also weiterhin in Figma (oder mit einem Figma-Plugin) erstellen. Sobald etwas in Figma existiert, kann ich es auslesen und in Code umsetzen oder mit Code verbinden.

---

In der [Figma MCP Doku](https://developers.figma.com/docs/figma-mcp-server/tools-and-prompts/#generate_figma_design) stehen zwei Tools, mit denen **doch** etwas in Figma/FigJam entsteht:

---

## 1. **`generate_figma_design`** (Design aus Live-UI)

Damit können **Design-Layer aus deiner laufenden Web-App** in Figma erzeugt werden:

- **Neue** Figma-Design-Dateien
- **Bestehende** Figma-Design-Dateien (mit Schreibrechten)
- **Zwischenablage** (zum Einfügen in eine beliebige Datei)

**Ablauf laut Doku:**

1. Remote Figma MCP Server einrichten.
2. Den Client z.B. so anweisen:
   - *"Start a local server for my app and capture the UI in a new Figma file."*
   - *"Capture the UI in &lt;Figma file URL&gt;"*
   - *"Capture the UI to my clipboard."*
3. Der Client startet den Server, injiziert das Capture-Skript und öffnet ein Browser-Fenster.
4. Über die Capture-Toolbar: **Entire screen** oder **Select element** wählen und die gewünschte UI nach Figma (oder Clipboard) schicken.

**Einschränkungen (laut Doku):**

- Nur **Remote** Figma MCP Server (nicht Desktop).
- Unterstützte Clients: **Claude Code, Codex by OpenAI** – ob Cursor denselben Remote-Server und das Tool anbietet, müsste man prüfen.
- Es geht um **Capture von Live-UI**, nicht um „von null zeichnen“ in Figma.

---

## 2. **`generate_diagram`** (FigJam-Diagramme aus Mermaid)

- Nimmt **Mermaid-Syntax** (Flowchart, Gantt, State, Sequence etc.) und erzeugt daraus ein **FigJam-Diagramm**.
- Du kannst auch in natürlicher Sprache beschreiben, was für ein Diagramm du willst; der Agent kann die Mermaid-Syntax erzeugen und dann `generate_diagram` aufrufen.

---

**Kurzfassung:**  
Technisch **kann** der Figma-MCP also Designs in Figma erzeugen – einmal durch **UI-Capture** (`generate_figma_design`) und einmal durch **FigJam-Diagramme** (`generate_diagram`). Ob **in deiner Cursor-Installation** diese Tools ankommen, hängt davon ab, ob du den **Remote** Figma MCP Server nutzt und ob Figma/Cursor sie dort anbieten. Im Projekt-MCP-Ordner tauchen `generate_figma_design` und `generate_diagram` in den Tool-Definitionen nicht auf – das kann bedeuten, dass das Cursor-Plugin nur einen Teil der Tools einbindet.

Wenn du willst, können wir als Nächstes prüfen, ob bei dir der Remote-Server konfiguriert ist und ob diese Tools in Cursor sichtbar sind.

Du hast den Figma MCP Server hinzugefügt. So geht’s weiter:

---

## 1. **Authentifizierung (Remote-Server)**

Wenn du den **Remote** Figma MCP Server nutzt (nicht nur die Desktop-App), muss er einmal authentifiziert werden:

- In Cursor: MCP-Tools aufrufen und das Tool **`mcp_auth`** für den Figma-Server ausführen (ohne Parameter).
- Oder in den Cursor-Einstellungen prüfen, ob beim Figma MCP ein Login-Link oder Hinweis zur Anmeldung bei Figma erscheint.

Ohne Auth funktionieren z.B. `get_design_context` oder `get_screenshot` mit Figma-URLs nicht.

---

## 2. **Was du jetzt machen kannst**

| Ziel | So geht’s |
|------|-----------|
| **Figma-Design → Code** | Figma-URL mit `?node-id=…` hier einfügen und z.B. schreiben: *„Implementiere dieses Design“* oder *„Baue diese Komponente nach“.* |
| **Figma Desktop** | Figma Desktop öffnen, Frame/Komponente auswählen, hier schreiben: *„Implementiere meine aktuelle Auswahl“* – dann nutze ich die Auswahl. |
| **Design mit Code verbinden** | *„Code Connect“* oder *„Verknüpfe diese Figma-Komponente mit Code“* (mit URL oder Auswahl in Figma Desktop). |
| **Projekt-Regeln für Figma** | *„Erstelle Design-System-Regeln für dieses Projekt“* – dann richte ich z.B. `.cursor/rules/figma-design-system.mdc` ein. |
| **FigJam-Diagramm** | *„Erstelle ein Flowchart/Sequenzdiagramm für … mit dem Figma MCP generate_diagram“* – dann kann ein FigJam-Diagramm aus der Beschreibung erzeugt werden. |

---

## 3. **Schnelltest**

Zum Prüfen, ob alles läuft:

1. **Mit Figma-URL:**  
   Einen Link zu einem Frame/Komponente einfügen (mit `node-id`) und schreiben:  
   *„Zeig mir den Design-Kontext für diesen Figma-Link.“*

2. **Mit Figma Desktop:**  
   In Figma etwas auswählen und schreiben:  
   *„Was ist in meiner aktuellen Figma-Auswahl?“*

Wenn einer der beiden Wege funktioniert, ist der Server eingebunden und du kannst die anderen Punkte aus der Tabelle nutzen.

Wenn du willst, kannst du mir als Nächstes eine Figma-URL (mit `node-id`) oder eine kurze Beschreibung geben – dann gehen wir Schritt für Schritt durch (z.B. „dieses Design implementieren“ oder „Code Connect einrichten“).

  "markdown": "Remote server | Developer Docs\n\nOn this page\n\nnote\n\nNote: You must use a code editor or application that supports MCP servers, such as VS Code, Cursor, or Claude Code. For a complete list of supported MCP clients, see the [Figma MCP Catalog](https://www.figma.com/mcp-catalog/).\n\nThe Figma MCP server brings Figma directly into your workflow by providing important design information and context to AI agents generating code from Figma design files.\n\nMCP servers are part of a standardized interface for AI agents to interact with data sources using the [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/).\n\nFigma's remote MCP server makes it possible to connect directly to your Figma files without needing to install Figma's desktop app. You can capture live UI to Figma Design and bring design context into your workflow wherever you're working.\n\nWith the server enabled, you can:\n\n- Keep your design system components consistent with Code Connect: Boost output quality by reusing your actual components. Code Connect keeps your generated code consistent with your codebase.\n- Retrieve Make resources: [Gather code resources from Make files](https://developers.figma.com/docs/figma-mcp-server/bringing-make-context-to-your-agent/) and provide them to the LLM as context. This can help as you move from prototype to production application.\n- Extract design context: Pull in variables, components, and layout data directly into your IDE. This is especially useful for design systems and component-based workflows.\n- Generate code from selected frames: Select a Figma frame and turn it into code. Great for product teams building new flows or iterating on app features.\n- Generate designs from live UI: Use a supported MCP client to capture the live UI of your web app or site and send it to new and existing Figma files or your clipboard. (Claude Code, Codex only)\n\nTo use this server, you'll need to sign in through Figma's OAuth authentication flow. Once set up, the remote server gives you a seamless way to access Figma data and integrate it with your tools.\n\n## Enabling the remote MCP server​\n\nnote\n\nThis documentation covers the installation steps for several of our supported MCP clients. For a list of install guides for supported MCP clients, see the [Guide to the Figma MCP server](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/32132100833559#h_01K25F7RBRZKCATVJHNXCS6KXW).\n\nFollow the steps for your MCP client:\n\n- VS Code\n- Cursor\n- Codex by OpenAI\n- Claude Code\n\n### Claude Code​\n\nRun the following command in your terminal to add the Figma MCP to Claude Code:&#96;claude mcp add --transport http figma https://mcp.figma.com/mcp&#96;\n\ntip\n\nTip: To make your Figma MCP server available across all projects, install it with the&#96;--scope user&#96; flag:&#96;claude mcp add --scope user --transport http figma https://mcp.figma.com/mcp&#96;\n\nBy default, Claude Code uses local scope, so the server is only available in the current project. Applying the user scope means you don't have to reinstall the server for each of your projects.\n\nStart a new instance of Claude Code.\n\nType&#96;/mcp&#96; in Claude to manage your MCP Servers and select figma.\n\nSelect Authenticate.\n\nClick Allow Access.\n\nBack in Claude code, you should see:&#96;Authentication successful. Connected to figma&#96;\n\nConfirm your MCP Server is connected using&#96;/mcp&#96; command in Claude Code.\n\nStart prompting!\n\n### Codex​\n\n#### Using the Codex app:​\n\n[Install the Codex app.](https://developers.openai.com/codex/app/)\n\nIn the upper-left corner of the Codex app, click Skills.\n\nIn the list of skills, click + next to the Figma skill.\n\nIn the bottom left corner of the Codex app, click Settings, and then MCP servers.\n\nIn the list of recommended servers, click Install and authenticate for the Figma server. When you're prompted, authorize the server.\n\nStart prompting Codex to [use the tools](https://developers.figma.com/docs/figma-mcp-server/tools-and-prompts/) provided by the Figma MCP server.\n\n#### Using the Codex CLI:​\n\n[Install the Codex CLI.](https://developers.openai.com/codex/cli/)\n\nIn your terminal, run the following command:&#96;codex mcp add figma --url https://mcp.figma.com/mcp&#96;\n\nWhen prompted, authenticate the server.\n\nStart prompting Codex to [use the tools](https://developers.figma.com/docs/figma-mcp-server/tools-and-prompts/) provided by the Figma MCP server.\n\n### Cursor​\n\n1. Start prompting!\n2. Allow access\n3. Click Open.\n4. Click Connect next to Figma to begin the authentication process.\n5. Click Install under 'Install MCP Server?'\n6. Click the [Figma MCP server deep link](cursor://anysphere.cursor-deeplink/mcp/install?name=Figma&amp;amp;config=eyJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL21jcC5maWdtYS5jb20vbWNwIn0%3D). This will open the MCP configuration in Cursor.\n\n### VS Code​\n\nUse the shortcut ⌘ Shift P, and then:\n\n- Select MCP: Open Workspace Folder MCP Configuration to use the Figma MCP server just inside your current workspace.\n- Select MCP: Open User Configuration to use the Figma MCP server globally.\n\nnote\n\nNote: If it doesn't exist already, you're prompted to create an&#96;mcp.json&#96; file.\n\nIn the&#96;mcp.json&#96; file, paste the following code:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n{\n   \"inputs\": [],\n   \"servers\": {\n     \"figma\": {\n       \"url\": \"https://mcp.figma.com/mcp\",\n       \"type\": \"http\"\n     }\n   }\n }\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nClick on Start (above the MCP server name).\n\nAllow Access.\n\nStart prompting!\n\n## Using the MCP server​\n\nThe MCP server introduces a set of tools that help LLMs bring your live UI to Figma Design and get design context from Figma. Once connected, you can start prompting your MCP client.\n\nFor a complete list of tools and examples, see [Tools and Prompts](https://developers.figma.com/docs/figma-mcp-server/tools-and-prompts/).\n\n### Example: Send UI to Figma​\n\nnote\n\nNote: Currently available for Claude Code and Codex by OpenAI only.\n\nSending the live UI of your web app or site to Figma is done through conversation with your MCP client:\n\n1. Let your client know when you're finished. Your client will give you a link to open the Figma file that was created.\n2. Use the capture toolbar to capture pages, elements, and states of your web app or site.\n3. Follow the steps your client provides. Your client will open a browser window for you, or give you a link to follow.\n4. Prompt your MCP client: \"Start a local server for my app and capture the UI in a new Figma file.”\n\n### Example: Get design context​\n\nGetting design context and code from files is link-based. To use it:\n\nCopy the link to a frame or layer in Figma\n\nPrompt your client to help you implement the design at the selected URL\n\nnote\n\nNote: Your client won't be able to navigate to the selected URL, but it will extract the node-id that is required for the MCP server to identify which object to return information about.\n\n## Improving the MCP server output​\n\nFor the best output, we recommend setting up rules to guide the agent. To get you started, we have some [helpful example rules](https://developers.figma.com/docs/figma-mcp-server/add-custom-rules/#rules-to-ensure-consistently-good-output).\n\nEnabling the remote MCP server\n\n- VS Code\n- Cursor\n- Codex\n- Claude Code\n\nUsing the MCP server\n\n- Example: Get design context\n- Example: Send UI to Figma\n\nWas this page helpful?\n\n[Leave us feedback](https://form.asana.com/?k=6r2Tos6p01DyVKGLeYJByg&amp;amp;d=10497086658021)\n\n---\n\n- [GitHub Samples](https://github.com/figma/widget-samples)\n- [Discord Server](https://discord.gg/xzQhe2Vcvx)\n- [Community Forum](https://forum.figma.com/)\n- Improving the MCP server output"
}

  "markdown": "Tools and prompts | Developer Docs\n\nOn this page\n\nThe Figma MCP server provides the following tools:\n\n- &#96;send_code_connect_mappings&#96;: A Figma-prompted tool used after calling&#96;get_code_connect_suggestions&#96; to confirm the suggested Code Connect mappings\n- &#96;get_code_connect_suggestions&#96;: A Figma-prompted tool call to find suggestions for mapping Figma node IDs to their corresponding code components in your codebase using Code Connect\n- &#96;whoami&#96;(remote only): Returns the identity of the user that's authenticated to Figma\n- &#96;generate_diagram&#96;: Generates a FigJam diagram from Mermaid syntax\n- &#96;get_figjam&#96;: Converts FigJam diagrams (such as app architecture workflows) to XML\n- &#96;get_metadata&#96;: Returns a sparse XML representation of your selection that contains basic properties such as layer IDs, names, types, position and sizes\n- &#96;create_design_system_rules&#96;: Creates a rule file that provide agents with the right context to translate designs into frontend code\n- &#96;get_screenshot&#96;: Allows the agent to take a screenshot of your selection\n- &#96;add_code_connect_map&#96;: Adds a mapping between a Figma node ID and its corresponding code component in your codebase\n- &#96;get_code_connect_map&#96;: Retrieves a mapping between Figma node IDs and their corresponding code components in your codebase\n- &#96;get_variable_defs&#96;: Returns the variables and styles used in your Figma selection\n- &#96;get_design_context&#96;: Get the design context for a layer or selection\n- &#96;generate_figma_design&#96;(specific clients only, remote only): Generates design layers from interfaces\n\n## generate_figma_design (remote only)​\n\nnote\n\nNote:\n\n- This tool is exempt from the standard rate limits for the Figma MCP server.\n- Remote Figma MCP server only.\n- Supported clients: Claude Code, Codex by OpenAI.\n\nSupported file types: Figma Design\n\n&#96;generate_figma_design&#96; lets you prompt your MCP client to send live UI for your web apps and sites as design layers to:\n\n- Your clipboard\n- Existing Figma Design files\n- New Figma Design files\n\n&#96;generate_figma_design&#96; respects your seat type when creating or editing files. New files are created in your team or organization drafts. For existing files, you must have edit permissions for the file.\n\nTo get started:\n\n[Set up the remote Figma MCP server.](https://developers.figma.com/docs/figma-mcp-server/remote-server-installation/) Follow the steps for your MCP client.\n\nPrompt your client to capture your interface to Figma. For example:\n\n- To your clipboard: “Start a local server for my app and use the Figma MCP server to capture the UI to my clipboard.”\n- To an existing file: “Start a local server for my app and capture the UI in.” If the URL doesn’t correspond to a Figma Design file, the agent defaults to creating a new file instead.\n- To a new file: “Start a local server for my app and capture the UI in a new Figma file.”\n\nYour client should start the server, inject the necessary script for the capture, and open a browser window. For new files, your client will ask you what Figma organization or team you want to use for the capture.\n\nAn initial capture happens when the browser window first opens.\n\nIn the browser window, use the capture toolbar to send specific parts and states of the UI to your Figma file or clipboard:\n\n- Click Select element to capture a specific element in the interface.\n- Click Entire screen to capture the current full state of your interface.\n\nWhen you’re done capturing your UI:\n\n- You can also prompt your MCP client to complete the process.\n- If you’re capturing UI to your clipboard, paste the design layers into a Figma file of your choice.\n- If you’re capturing UI to a Figma Design file, click Open file.\n\nTips:\n\nIf you or your client have already started a local server, you can exclude the “Start a local server for my app…” portion of the example prompts. Generally, your client may infer that a local server needs to be available, but it can help to be specific the first time.\n\nIf you’re capturing UI multiple times in a conversation, your client will usually infer that you want to send the UI to the same file.\n\nFor example, suppose you’re capturing the interface of a site with multiple pages. If you prompt “Capture the login page to,” you can subsequently prompt things like “Capture checkout to the same file” or even simply, “Also capture the account screen.”\n\nGenerally, your client will know you want to use the same Figma file, or will you ask you to confirm.\n\nWhen you’re capturing UI to an existing file, your client can suggest recent files you’ve created or edited as options.\n\nIf you’re trying to capture your UI from a live web app or site, you can prompt your client to use a tool like Playwright to inject the required script. For local UI, this isn’t necessary.\n\n## get_design_context​\n\nSupported file types: Figma Design, Figma Make\n\nUse the MCP server to get the [design context](https://developers.figma.com/docs/figma-mcp-server/server-returning-web-code/#about-the-get_design_context-tool) for a layer or your selection in Figma. By default, the output is React + Tailwind, but you can customize it through your prompt.\n\nSuggested prompts:\n\nChange the framework\n\n- &#96;generate my Figma selection in iOS&#96;\n- &#96;generate my Figma selection in plain HTML + CSS&#96;\n- &#96;generate my Figma selection in Vue&#96;\n\nUse your components\n\n- *Tip: set up Code Connect for best code reuse results. Code Connect lets you set up multiple connections per Figma Library. You can map, for example, both your React and SwiftUI code to your Figma components. The Desktop MCP server will use the Code Connect mapping you have selected in Dev Mode. To control which Code Connect mappings are sent via the Remote MCP Server, instruct your agent to set the&#96;clientFrameworks&#96; tool call paramater to the exact Code Connect label you have set up for your mappings, e.g..&#96;React&#96;,&#96;SwiftUI&#96;.\n- &#96;generate my Figma selection using components from src/components/ui&#96;\n\nCombine both\n\n- &#96;generate my Figma selection using components from src/ui and style with Tailwind&#96;\n\nnote\n\nNote: Selection-based prompting only works with the desktop MCP server. The remote server requires a link to a frame or layer to extract context.\n\n## get_variable_defs​\n\nSupported file types: Figma Design\n\nReturns the variables and styles used in your Figma selection (such as colors, spacing, typography).\n\nYou can ask it to:\n\nList all tokens used\n\n- &#96;get the variables used in my Figma selection&#96;\n\nFocus on a specific type\n\n- &#96;what color and spacing variables are used in my Figma selection?&#96;\n\nGet both names and values\n\n- &#96;list the variable names and their values used in my Figma selection&#96;\n\n## get_code_connect_map​\n\nSupported file types: Figma Design\n\nRetrieves a mapping between Figma node IDs and their corresponding code components in your codebase. Specifically, it returns an object where each key is a Figma node ID, and the value contains:\n\n- &#96;codeConnectName&#96;: The name of the component in your codebase.\n- &#96;codeConnectSrc&#96;: The location of the component in your codebase (e.g., a file path or URL).\n\nThis mapping is used to connect Figma design elements directly to their React (or other framework) implementations, enabling seamless design-to-code workflows and ensuring that the correct components are used for each part of the design. If a Figma node is connected to a code component, this function helps you identify and use the exact component in your project.\n\n## add_code_connect_map​\n\nSupported file types: Figma Design\n\nAdds a mapping between a Figma node ID and its corresponding code component in your codebase. Setting up these mappings will improve the output quality of design-to-code worksflows and help you identify and use the exact component in your project.\n\n## get_screenshot​\n\nSupported file types: Figma Design, FigJam\n\nAllows the agent to take a screenshot of your selection. This helps preserve layout fidelity in the generated code. Recommended to keep on (only turn off if you're concerned about token limits).\n\n## create_design_system_rules​\n\nSupported file types: No file context required\n\nA tool for creating a rule file that provide agents with the right context to translate designs into high-quality, codebase-aware frontend code. It helps ensure alignment with your design system and tech stack, improving the relevance and accuracy of generated output.\n\nRun this tool and make sure the result is saved to the correct&#96;rules/&#96; or&#96;instructions/&#96; path so your agent can access it during code generation.\n\n## get_metadata​\n\nSupported file types: Figma Design\n\nReturns a sparse XML representation of your selection containing just basic properties such as the layer IDs, names, types, position and sizes. This is an outline that your Agent can then break down and call&#96;get_design_context&#96; on to retrieve only the styling information of the design it needs. Useful for very large designs where&#96;get_design_context&#96; produces output with a large context size. It also works with multiple selections or the whole page if you don't select anything.\n\n## get_figjam​\n\nSupported file types: FigJam\n\nThis tool returns metadata for FigJam diagrams in XML format, similar to&#96;get_metadata&#96;. In addition to returning basic properties like layer IDs, names, types, positions, and sizes, it also includes screenshots of the nodes.\n\n## generate_diagram​\n\nSupported file types: No file context required\n\nGenerates a FigJam diagram from Mermaid syntax. This tool accepts Mermaid diagram definitions and converts them into interactive FigJam diagrams that can be edited and shared.\n\nYou do not have to provide Mermaid syntax yourself. Instead, you can prompt the agent to create diagrams by describing the desired diagram in natural language. The agent will then generate the appropriate Mermaid syntax and use the generate_diagram tool to create the FigJam diagram.\n\nTo ensure that the agent uses the Figma MCP&#96;generate_diagram&#96; tool, you can include the directive \"Use the Figma MCP generate_diagram tool\" in your prompt. However, the agent will likely use the tool automatically when it determines that a diagram is needed based on your request.\n\nSupported diagram types:\n\n- Sequence diagram\n- State diagram\n- Gantt chart\n- Flowchart\n\nSuggested prompts:\n\nGenerate a diagram from a description\n\n- &#96;generate a sequence diagram for the payment processing system using the Figma MCP generate_diagram tool&#96;\n- &#96;generate a gantt chart for the project timeline using the Figma MCP generate_diagram tool&#96;\n- &#96;create a flowchart for the user authentication flow using the Figma MCP generate_diagram tool&#96;\n\nConvert existing Mermaid syntax\n\n- &#96;create a diagram from this mermaid syntax: ...&#96;\n\n## whoami (remote only)​\n\nSupported file types: No file context required\n\nThis tool returns the identity of the user that's authenticated to Figma, including:\n\n- The seat type the user has on each plan\n- All of the plans the user belongs to\n- The user's email address\n\n## get_code_connect_suggestions​\n\nSupported file types: Figma Design\n\nA tool call prompted by Figma to detect and suggest mappings of Figma components to code components in your codebase using Code Connect.\n\n## send_code_connect_mappings​\n\nSupported file types: Figma Design\n\nA tool call prompted by Figma to confirm the Code Connect mappings after calling&#96;get_code_connect_suggestions&#96;.\n\n- send_code_connect_mappings\n- get_code_connect_suggestions\n- whoami (remote only)\n- generate_diagram\n- get_figjam\n- get_metadata\n- create_design_system_rules\n- get_screenshot\n- add_code_connect_map\n- get_code_connect_map\n- get_variable_defs\n- get_design_context\n- generate_figma_design (remote only)\n\nWas this page helpful?\n\n[Leave us feedback](https://form.asana.com/?k=6r2Tos6p01DyVKGLeYJByg&amp;amp;d=10497086658021)\n\n---\n\n- [GitHub Samples](https://github.com/figma/widget-samples)\n- [Discord Server](https://discord.gg/xzQhe2Vcvx)\n- [Community Forum](https://forum.figma.com/)"
